id: runbook-pipeline-failure
title: "Runbook: Pipeline Failure"
sidebar_label: "🔥 Runbook: Pipeline Failure"
---

import { MetaBlock, RunbookStep, InfoPanel, ApiTryIt } from '@site/src/components/PageLayouts';

<MetaBlock
  owner="Platform Team"
  team="SRE"
  status="live"
  lastUpdated="2026-06-02"
  reviewedBy="Priya Shah"
  tags={['runbook', 'incident', 'pipeline', 'sre', 'p1']}
/>

<InfoPanel type="danger" title="Severity: P1 — Follow steps in order. Do NOT skip.">
  This runbook covers complete pipeline failures where <code>POST /generate</code> returns
  5xx errors or hangs indefinitely. Work through every step in sequence before escalating.
  Each step is self-contained — run the command, check the expected output, then proceed.
</InfoPanel>

---

## Pre-flight Checks

<RunbookStep step={1} title="Verify the FastAPI server is responding" severity="critical"
  command="curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq .">

**Expected output:**
```json
{ "status": "ok", "slidev_running": true }
```

If you get `Connection refused` → the server is down, go to **Step 2**.
If you get a JSON body but `slidev_running: false` → Slidev process died, go to **Step 6**.

</RunbookStep>

<RunbookStep step={2} title="Check for zombie processes holding port 8000" severity="high"
  command="lsof -i :8000 | grep LISTEN">

If you see a PID in the output, a stale process is blocking the port. Kill it:

```bash
kill -9 <PID>
```

Then restart the server (Step 3). If `lsof` returns empty, the server simply never started — proceed to Step 3.

</RunbookStep>

<RunbookStep step={3} title="Restart the pipeline server" severity="high"
  command="cd /path/to/docusaurus-to-slidev && python server.py &">

Wait **5 seconds**, then hit `/health` again:

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq .
```

If it still returns `Connection refused`, check environment variables (**Step 4**).
If it returns 200 OK, proceed to **Step 5** to test a live generate.

</RunbookStep>

<RunbookStep step={4} title="Verify environment variables are set" severity="high"
  command="echo $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | cut -c1-12">

You should see `sk-ant-api03`. If blank, re-export and restart:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-...
python server.py &
```

**For Databricks providers — also check:**
```bash
echo $DATABRICKS_HOST
echo $DATABRICKS_TOKEN | cut -c1-8
echo $DATABRICKS_ENDPOINT
```

All three must be non-empty if `LLM_PROVIDER=databricks`.

</RunbookStep>

<InfoPanel type="note" title="LLM Provider mock mode">
  If <code>ANTHROPIC_API_KEY</code> is missing, the pipeline falls back to <strong>mock mode</strong>
  (no real LLM calls). Mock mode will not error — but slides will contain placeholder text.
  Check <code>pipeline.log</code> for the line <code>LLM mode: mock</code> to confirm.
</InfoPanel>

---

## Live Request Test

<RunbookStep step={5} title="Fire a minimal generate request" severity="medium">

Use the tester below to confirm the pipeline accepts requests end-to-end:

</RunbookStep>

<ApiTryIt
  method="POST"
  url="http://localhost:8000/generate"
  description="Test pipeline — generates slides for the intro doc"
  body={{ url_path: '/docs/intro' }}
/>

<InfoPanel type="tip" title="What a healthy response looks like">
  Status <code>200</code> with a body containing <code>slides_url</code>, <code>slide_count</code>,
  and <code>generation_time_ms</code>. Anything else — capture the body and attach it to your
  incident ticket.
</InfoPanel>

---

## Slidev Process Issues

<RunbookStep step={6} title="Check for port conflict on :3030" severity="medium"
  command="lsof -i :3030">

If another process owns `:3030`, the pipeline generates the slide file but silently fails to launch Slidev. Kill the offender:

```bash
kill -9 <PID>
```

Then trigger another generate request. The server auto-restarts Slidev on each successful pipeline run.

</RunbookStep>

<RunbookStep step={7} title="Manually start Slidev to isolate the failure" severity="medium"
  command="cd output/slides && npx slidev slides.md --port 3030 --open">

If Slidev fails to start here with an error, the issue is in the generated `slides.md` file — likely malformed frontmatter or a broken Mermaid block. Inspect the file:

```bash
head -30 output/slides/slides.md
```

Look for:
- Missing closing `---` on frontmatter
- Leftover JSX tags like `<ComponentName>`
- Unclosed fenced code blocks ` ``` `

</RunbookStep>

---

## Log Inspection

<RunbookStep step={8} title="Pull the last 100 lines from the pipeline log" severity="low"
  command="tail -100 pipeline.log | grep -E 'ERROR|WARN|Exception'">

Common errors and what they mean:

| Log snippet | Root cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `anthropic.AuthenticationError` | API key invalid or expired | Rotate key, re-export |
| `FileNotFoundError: slides.md` | `output/slides/` dir missing | `mkdir -p output/slides` |
| `Port 3030 already in use` | Stale Slidev process | `kill $(lsof -ti:3030)` |
| `JSONDecodeError` in response | LangGraph state corruption | Restart server; check Python ≥3.11 |
| `LLM timeout after 30s` | Anthropic API slow | Retry; check `status.anthropic.com` |

</RunbookStep>

<RunbookStep step={9} title="Check the Docusaurus dev server is accessible" severity="low"
  command="curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3000/">

Must return `200`. If not, the Docusaurus server is down — restart it:

```bash
cd docs-site && npm start
```

This does not affect the pipeline itself but breaks the Generate Slides button UI.

</RunbookStep>

---

## Escalation

<InfoPanel type="warning" title="Escalate if all 9 steps pass but failure persists">

Collect the following before paging the on-call engineer:

1. **Error output:** `tail -100 pipeline.log`
2. **Health check:** `curl http://localhost:8000/health`
3. **The failing `url_path`** that triggered the 5xx
4. **Python version:** `python --version`
5. **Node version:** `node --version`

Post in **#platform-oncall** with subject: `[P1] POST /generate returning 5xx — runbook exhausted`.

</InfoPanel>

| Escalation tier | Contact | When |
|---|---|---|
| L1 On-call | `#platform-oncall` Slack | Steps 1–5 failed |
| L2 Platform Lead | Page via PagerDuty | Steps 6–9 also failed |
| L3 Vendor (Anthropic) | `support.anthropic.com` | API-level errors confirmed |

---

## Post-Resolution Checklist

Once the pipeline is healthy again:

- [ ] Confirm `/health` returns `{"status":"ok","slidev_running":true}`
- [ ] Trigger a generate on `/docs/intro` and verify deck opens on `:3030`
- [ ] Document the root cause in `#platform-incidents`
- [ ] Add new failure modes to the log table in Step 8 if not already there
- [ ] File a follow-up ticket if a permanent fix is needed
